content:
```mermaid flowchart TB classDef hi fill:#ffe5e5,stroke:#cf222e,color:#5c0007 classDef neutral fill:#f6f8fa,stroke:#57606a classDef low fill:#d4f1d4,stroke:#1a7f37,color:#0a3e1f title[Long-term CV Safety: AHA 2024 vs Ramadan Observational] subgraph AHA [AHA 2024 NHANES Analysis] A1[Population: ~20,078 U.S. adults]:::neutral A2[Exposure: 2-day dietary recall
NHANES 2003-2018]:::neutral A3[Follow-up: median 8 y]:::neutral A4[Finding: 91% higher CV mortality
with eating window <8h]:::hi A5[Subgroup: existing CVD
66% higher CV death
with 8-10h window]:::hi A6[Peer-review: PMID 40849219
with caveats]:::neutral end subgraph Ramadan [Ramadan Observational Literature] R1[Population: hundreds of millions
of Muslim adults]:::neutral R2[Exposure: religiously-defined
annual dawn-to-sunset fast]:::neutral R3[Follow-up: annual cycles]:::neutral R4[Finding: CV event incidence
during Ramadan similar to non-fasting]:::low R5[Pooled effects on weight, BP,
waist circumference: modest favorable]:::low end subgraph Critique [Methodological critique] C1[Reverse causation: people who self-restrict
may have pre-existing illness] C2[NHANES 2003-2018 predates intentional IF;
respondents not intentional IF adopters] C3[2-day dietary recall inadequate
for long-term pattern classification] end A4 --> C1 A4 --> C2 A4 --> C3 R4 -.contradicts.-> A4 ```
